title: We Are All Wanting to Fit In — Bee vs Ant + 3 Dearborn Hall
html:
TL;DR

  • OSU first-week-of-classes brain dump (Sep 22, 2021). Opens with the load-bearing line: "We are all wanting to 'fit' in. Afraid to not fit in. Never have an almost" — explicit identity-formation note about the social anxiety of arriving at college.
  • "Never have an almost" — possible self-instruction: don't half-commit. Reads as a manifesto-style fragment.
  • Mid-note observation about a new dorm acquaintance: "3 Dearborn hall 118. Plays melee. People drain him, but loves being around people. Burrito steak sour cream cheese hot sauce. Would rather be a bee than an ant." — anthropological capture of a roommate or floormate, with the bee-vs-ant choice doing real work as a mini-personality test.
  • Civic-anxiety beat: "America infrastructure is graded a d-" — same brain that's been tracking macro since 2020 03 24 exogenous event 911 now reading Biden-era infrastructure debate.
  • Closes with a non-sequitur half-joke: "Long term goal of throwing a doge dog at his head" — the Grants Pass sense of humor traveling with him to Corvallis.
  • This note is the cleanest articulation of the identity-formation tension that fuels much of his fall-2021 / freshman material — including 2021 10 11 rick and morty brainstorming and 2021 11 05 ceos of the future.

Key quotes

"We are all wanting to 'fit' in. Afraid to not fit in. Never have an almost."

"Would rather be a bee than an ant."

Open questions

  • The "3 Dearborn hall 118" person — name not captured. Possibly Cam Jipp / one of the early Corvallis dorm friends. Worth tracing in a densify.
  • "Never have an almost" — does this resurface as a maxim? Worth grepping the corpus.

Connections

osu · corvallis or · grants pass teen era · cam jipp · 2021 10 11 rick and morty brainstorming · 2021 11 05 ceos of the future · 2020 03 24 exogenous event 911
